agent2: thanks for the heads up. My debug-logging changes to streaming_listener.py: (1) __init__ gets two NEW trailing params debug: bool=False, debug_logger: logging.Logger|None=None (appended after allow_reuse), plus self.debug/self._logger set at end of __init__ body. (2) import logging added at top. (3) In receive(): guarded debug.log lines added at the allow_reuse reset block (right after existing state-clearing, not changing behavior), at cache_hit start-detection (~line 111-115), at the field_start_queue start-detection block, at the rolling end-check (regex_match) inside 'if self.stream_start', and right before 'return StreamResponse(...)' for emit-chunk. (4) flush() refactored internally to compute result once then log then return (single return path) but same behavior/output. I'm not changing detection algorithms or the reset semantics (still gated by allow_reuse, not unconditional). If your tick()/idle_timeout resets state unconditionally that's a separate code path from mine so should merge fine as long as we don't both edit the exact same lines - happy to co-locate/merge if needed, just let me know before you finalize the reset block edits.

Done with my edits to streaming_listener.py. Summary of exact changes so you can append cleanly: (1) added 'import time' at top (line 2). (2) __init__ signature now ends '...allow_reuse: bool = False, idle_timeout_s: float | None = None,' — please append your debug/debug_logger params after idle_timeout_s to keep a clean append order. (3) __init__ body: added self.idle_timeout_s and self._last_chunk_ts = None as the last two lines before the closing of __init__ (after self.allow_reuse = allow_reuse). (4) In receive(), right after the try/except that sets chunk_message, added:
    if chunk_message:
        self._last_chunk_ts = time.time()
  before the 'if chunk_message and start_identifier in chunk_message:' line — feel free to add debug logs around this without removing it. (5) Added a new tick(self, now_ts=None) method between receive() and flush() — self-contained, shouldn't collide with your logging additions. I did NOT touch the allow_reuse reset block inside receive() (lines ~86-101) or flush() bodies, so your logging additions there are clear. Going to run tests now; will ping if I touch anything else.
